LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT GREEN APPLE, INC.'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. THE MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED FOR GOOD CAUSE SHOWN PURSUANT TO CCP 473(B). THE MOTION WAS FILED LESS THAN 6 MONTHS AFTER DEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT HAS ESTABLISHED THROUGH THE DECLARATIONS OF KEN UNG, JANICE WONG, AND TONY C. HOH THAT ITS FAILURE TO RESPOND TIMELY TO THE COMPLAINT IS DUE TO MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE, AND EXCUSABLE NEGLECT; AND THAT THE MOTION WAS MADE WITHIN A REASONABLE TIME UNDER ALL OF THE CIRCUMSTANCES. DEFENDANT SHALL FILE THE PROPOSED ANSWER WITHIN TEN DAYS AFTER NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR# 5976.(302/MJM)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Apr-29-2015 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Dec-19-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Disqualify Harvey W. Stein And Law Offices Of Harvey W. Stein
Off Calendar Feb-10-2015 Advanced From Mar-25-15 To Feb-27-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-17-2015 Continued To May-04-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion To Disqualify Harvey W. Stein And Law Offices Of Harvey W. Stein
Off Calendar Feb-27-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. HARVEY W. STEIN, Tel No. (510) 763-6233 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. TONY C. HOH, Tel No. (415) 788-8810 appearing for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT GREEN APPLE, INC.'S Motion To Disqualify Harvey W. Stein And Law Offices Of Harvey W. Stein is denied. It is clear from the record that the Motion could and should have been made concurrently with or shortly after the hearing on the motion to set aside default. No such action was taken, nor did Defendant avail himself of the opportunity to do so until thirteen months after Ung was first served, six months after counsel was retained, six months after Ung's declaration was filed in support of his motion to set aside default, five days before Ung's deposition, and within a few weeks of trial. Stein's connection with the adversary party was revealed in Ung's Declaration filed six months ago in support of his motion to set aside default. Alternatively, the Court finds that no substantial relationship exists to warrant disqualification. Stein did not represent Ung in an individual capacity at his deposition, but only in a representative capacity as an officer of Icebee, and Ung revealed no confidential information to Stein during the Icebee litigation. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Paula J. Shi, CSR #13358 (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-13-2014 Set For Trial On Mar-23-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Oct-16-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Tony C. Hoh, Esq. of the Law Office of Tony C. Hoh, T:415.788.8810, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested on DEFENDANT GREEN APPLE, INC.'s MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. The motion to set aside default and default judgment is granted for good cause shown pursuant to CCP 473(b). The motion was filed less than 6 months after default judgment against defendant. Defendant has established through the declarations of Ken Ung, Janice Wong, and Tony C. Hoh that its failure to respond timely to the Complaint is due to mistake, inadvertence, and excusable neglect; and that the motion was made within a reasonable time under all of the circumstances. Defendant shall file the proposed Answer within ten days after notice of entry of this order. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
